title: Forcepoint Email Security DLP and Policy Blocks (KV SIEM Format)
dataSources: Forcepoint Email Security SIEM syslog in key-value form, `sourcetype=forcepoint:email:kv` (per SC4S / deployment convention)
app: Splunk Connect for Syslog classification for Forcepoint Email, or custom props for `forcepoint:email:kv`
spl:
index=email sourcetype="forcepoint:email:kv" earliest=-7d
| eval pol=coalesce(cs_policy, deviceProcessName, policy)
| eval reason=coalesce(exceptionReason, cs_reason, reason)
| where match(lower(reason),"(?i)dlp|confidential|encrypt|block|violation")
| stats count by suser, duser, pol, reason
| sort -count
| head 40
description: Forcepoint Email Security emits structured SIEM events when messages violate DLP, encryption, or acceptable-use policies. Monitoring blocked or redirected mail by sender, recipient, and policy supports insider risk and regulatory use cases.
value: Extends DLP monitoring to the primary channel employees still use for high-volume data transfer—corporate email.
implementation: Enable SIEM integration in Forcepoint Security Manager using syslog key-value format compatible with Splunk. Confirm field names against your appliance’s SIEM map (suser, duser, exceptionReason). Restrict index access to mail security and compliance roles. Integrate with phishing and BEC workflows when `reason` indicates impersonation.
visualization: Table (sender, recipient, policy), Timeline (block volume), Pie chart (policy mix).

references.title: Splunk Connect for Syslog — Forcepoint Email Security
